parameters.query: SELECT count(*)::int AS hit FROM agent_projects WHERE id = $1::uuid;

WITH hit AS (
  SELECT id, status, version, title, project_id, task_id, parent_spec_id, root_spec_id, created_at, updated_at, requirements, acceptance_criteria, constraints, dependencies, non_goals, open_questions, summary, body_markdown
  FROM agent_specifications
  WHERE idempotency_key IS NOT NULL AND idempotency_key = $1::text
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT (SELECT count(*) FROM hit)::int AS hits, (SELECT row_to_json(hit) FROM hit) AS row;

parameters.jsCode:
// On idempotency replay return ONLY the persisted row — never mix with a freshly parsed spec.
const row = ($json && $json.row) || $json;
return [{ json: {
  ok: true,
  spec_id: row.id,
  status: row.status,
  version: row.version,
  title: row.title,
  spec: {
    title: row.title,
    summary: row.summary,
    body_markdown: row.body_markdown,
    requirements: row.requirements,
    acceptance_criteria: row.acceptance_criteria,
    constraints: row.constraints,
    dependencies: row.dependencies,
    non_goals: row.non_goals,
    open_questions: row.open_questions
  },
  questions: row.open_questions || [],
  validation_issues: [],
  memory_id: null,
  replayed: true
} }];

parameters.query: INSERT INTO agent_memories (agent_name, memory_type, content, metadata) VALUES ('specification_agent', $1::text, $2::text, $3::jsonb) RETURNING id;

options.queryReplacement: ={{ [ ($('Parse + Validate Spec Output').first().json.status === 'reviewed' ? 'decision' : 'question_set'), ('Spec ' + $json.result.spec.id + ' ' + $json.result.spec.status + ' for project ' + $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id + ': ' + ($json.result.spec.title || 'Untitled')), JSON.stringify({ spec_id: $json.result.spec.id, version: $json.result.spec.version, project_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id, task_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.task_id, idempotency_key_present: !!$('Validate Input').first().json.idempotency_key }) ] }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { source: 'specification_agent', action: 'spec_written', spec_id: $('Create Spec Revision').first().json.result.spec.id, project_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id, status: $('Create Spec Revision').first().json.result.spec.status, title: $('Create Spec Revision').first().json.result.spec.title, version: $('Create Spec Revision').first().json.result.spec.version } }}

parameters.query: SELECT count(*)::int AS hit FROM agent_projects WHERE id = $1::uuid;

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: false, error: 'missing_project', detail: 'project_id not found in agent_projects' }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: false, error: 'db_unavailable', detail: 'create_specification_revision failed' }) }}

meta.version_note: Round 2 — adversarial review pass 1 closed (auth, SQLi, parameterised binding, schema validation, error paths, idempotency).

credentials_required.name: APEX Webhook Token
credentials_required.note: Bearer-style token validated by webhook authentication=headerAuth. Bind on import.

meta.system_prompt_drift_policy: The inline prompt in node 'Build Prompt' is a deliberately-tightened operational subset of the canonical AGEN-PRMPT-spec-system-prompt-v1.md. It encodes the schema, status enum, and the deterministic invariants the workflow validator can mechanically enforce (banned-word regex, required fields, status downgrade rules) — NOT the full ROLE BOUNDARY / TRUST BOUNDARY / FAILURE MODES sections. The deterministic post-validator in 'Parse + Validate Spec Output' enforces what the prompt cannot. Drift contract: when the canonical prompt's MEANING (what makes a spec reviewed-quality) changes, this inline prompt and the validator MUST be updated together; the prompt sha256 prefix above is recorded and import-time tooling SHOULD compare against the current canonical file.
meta.approve_action_owner: Project Manager Agent — call /webhook/project-manager with action=approve_spec. Spec Agent does NOT expose approve.
